title: Hieracium subumbelliforme
intro: Hieracium subumbelliforme, also known as the small yellow hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium subumbelliforme include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Hieracium subumbelliforme is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Hieracium subumbelliforme belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its composite inflorescence and the presence of achenes, which are typical features of the Asteraceae family. Within the family Asteraceae, Hieracium subumbelliforme is further classified as a member of the genus Hieracium, which includes about 1000 species of plants commonly known as hawkweeds. The genus Hieracium is a part of the tribe Senecioneae, which also includes other related plant species. The tribe Senecioneae is a subfamily of the Asteraceae family known as the Cichorioideae subfamily.
